Study of Market Mechanisms in Federal Student Loan Programs, , , which directed the Comptroller General and Secretary of Education, in consultation with a study group, to design and conduct a study to identify and evaluate means of establishing a market mechanism for the delivery of certain student loans made pursuant to title IV of the Higher Education Act ( et seq.) and to transmit a final report to congressional committees not later than , was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Department a Performance-Based Organization (hereafter referred to as the “PBO”) which shall be a discrete management unit responsible for managing the administrative and oversight functions supporting the programs authorized under subchapter IV of this chapter, as specified in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: to improve service to students and other participants in the student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV, including making those programs more understandable to students and their parents;

children.label: B
children.text: to reduce the costs of administering those programs;

children.label: C
children.text: to increase the accountability of the officials responsible for administering the operational aspects of these programs;

children.label: D
children.text: to provide greater flexibility in the management and administration of the Federal student financial assistance programs;

children.label: E
children.text: to integrate the information systems supporting the Federal student financial assistance programs;

children.label: F
children.text: to implement an open, common, integrated system for the delivery of student financial assistance under subchapter IV; and

children.label: G
children.text: to develop and maintain a student financial assistance system that contains complete, accurate, and timely data to ensure program integrity.

children.label: A
children.text: request the advice of, and work in cooperation with, the Chief Operating Officer in developing regulations, policies, administrative guidance, or procedures affecting the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV;

children.label: B
children.text: request cost estimates from the Chief Operating Officer for system changes required by specific policies proposed by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: the administration of the systems used to administer the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the updating of such systems to current technology.

children.label: i
children.text: the collection, processing, and transmission of data to students, institutions, lenders, State agencies, and other authorized parties;

children.label: ii
children.text: the design and technical specifications for software development and procurement for systems supporting the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV;

children.label: iii
children.text: all software and hardware acquisitions and all information technology contracts related to the administration and management of student financial assistance under subchapter IV;

children.label: iv
children.text: all aspects of contracting for the information and financial systems supporting the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV;

children.label: v
children.text: providing all customer service, training, and user support related to the administration of the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV; and

children.label: vi
children.text: ensuring the integrity of the Federal student financial assistance programs authorized under subchapter IV.

children.label: B
children.text: Annual development of a budget for the activities and functions of the PBO, in consultation with the Secretary, and for consideration and inclusion in the Department’s annual budget submission.

children.label: i
children.text: detecting common patterns of improper use of any system that processes payments on Federal Direct Loans or other Department information technology systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: maintaining a reporting system for contractors involved in the processing of payments on Federal Direct Loans in order to allow those contractors to alert the Secretary of potentially improper use of Department information technology systems;

children.label: iii
children.text: proactively contacting Federal student loan borrowers whose Federal student loan accounts demonstrate a likelihood of improper use in order to warn those borrowers of suspicious activity or potential fraud regarding their Federal student loan accounts; and

children.label: iv
children.text: providing clear and simple disclosures in communications with borrowers who are applying for or requesting assistance with Federal Direct Loan programs (including assistance or applications regarding income-driven repayment, forbearance, deferment, consolidation, rehabilitation, cancellation, and forgiveness) to ensure that borrowers are aware that the Department will never require borrowers to pay for such assistance or applications.

children.heading: Additional functions
children.label: 3 Additional functions
children.text: The Secretary may allocate to the PBO such additional functions as the Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer determine are necessary or appropriate to achieve the purposes of the PBO.

children.heading: Independence
children.label: 4 Independence
children.text: Subject to paragraph (1), in carrying out its functions, the PBO shall exercise independent control of its budget allocations and expenditures, personnel decisions and processes, procurements, and other administrative and management functions.

children.heading: Audits and review
children.label: 5 Audits and review
children.text: The PBO shall be subject to the usual and customary Federal audit procedures and to review by the Inspector General of the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer shall consult concerning the effects of policy, market, or other changes on the ability of the PBO to achieve the goals and objectives established in the performance plan described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Revisions to agreement
children.label: B Revisions to agreement
children.text: The Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer may revise the annual performance agreement described in subsection (d)(4) in light of policy, market, or other changes that occur after the Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer enter into the agreement.

children.heading: Performance plan, report, and briefing
children.label: c Performance plan, report, and briefing

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each year, the Secretary and Chief Operating Officer shall agree on, and make available to the public, a performance plan for the PBO for the succeeding 5 years that establishes measurable goals and objectives for the organization.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: In developing the 5-year performance plan and any revision to the plan, the Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer shall consult with students, institutions of higher education, Congress, lenders, the Advisory Committee on Student Financial Assistance, and other interested parties not less than 30 days prior to the implementation of the performance plan or revision.

children.heading: Improving service
children.label: i Improving service
children.text: Improving service to students and other participants in student financial aid programs authorized under under subchapter IV, including making those programs more understandable to students and their parents.1So in original.1

children.heading: Reducing costs
children.label: ii Reducing costs
children.text: Reducing the costs of administering those programs.

children.heading: Improvement and integration of support systems
children.label: iii Improvement and integration of support systems
children.text: Improving and integrating the systems that support those programs.

children.heading: Delivery and information system
children.label: iv Delivery and information system
children.text: Developing open, common, and integrated systems for programs authorized under under subchapter IV.1

children.heading: Other areas
children.label: v Other areas
children.text: Any other areas identified by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: An independent financial audit of the expenditures of both the PBO and the programs administered by the PBO.

children.label: B
children.text: Financial and performance requirements applicable to the PBO under the Chief Financial Officers Act of 1990 and the Government Performance and Results Act of 1993.

children.label: C
children.text: The results achieved by the PBO during the year relative to the goals established in the organization’s performance plan.

children.label: D
children.text: The evaluation rating of the performance of the Chief Operating Officer and senior managers under subsections (d)(4) and (e)(2), including the amounts of bonus compensation awarded to these individuals.

children.label: E
children.text: Recommendations for legislative and regulatory changes to improve service to students and their families, and to improve program efficiency and integrity.

children.label: F
children.text: Other such information as the Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall prescribe for performance based organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: regarding the degree of satisfaction with the delivery system; and

children.label: B
children.text: to seek suggestions on means to improve the delivery system.

children.heading: Briefing on enforcement of student loan provisions
children.label: 4 Briefing on enforcement of student loan provisions
A
children.text: the integrity of the student loan programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: that lenders and guaranty agencies are adhering to the requirements of subchapter IV.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The management of the PBO shall be vested in a Chief Operating Officer who shall be appointed by the Secretary to a term of not less than 3 and not more than 5 years, and compensated without regard to chapters 33, 51, and 53 of title 5. The appointment shall be made on the basis of demonstrated management ability and expertise in information technology, including experience with financial systems, and without regard to political affiliation or activity.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: 2 Reappointment
children.text: The Secretary may reappoint the Chief Operating Officer to subsequent terms of not less than 3 and not more than 5 years, so long as the performance of the Chief Operating Officer, as set forth in the performance agreement described in paragraph (4), is satisfactory.

children.label: A
children.text: the President; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary, for misconduct or failure to meet performance goals set forth in the performance agreement in paragraph (4).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each year, the Secretary and the Chief Operating Officer shall enter into an annual performance agreement, that shall set forth measurable organization and individual goals for the Chief Operating Officer.

children.heading: Transmittal
children.label: B Transmittal
children.text: The final agreement, and any revision to the final agreement, shall be transmitted to the authorizing committees, and made publicly available.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer is authorized to be paid at an annual rate of basic pay not to exceed the maximum rate of basic pay for the Senior Executive Service under , including any applicable locality-based comparability payment that may be authorized under section 5304(h)(2)(B) of such title. The compensation of the Chief Operating Officer shall be considered for purposes of to be the equivalent of that described under clause (ii) of section 207(c)(2)(A) of such title.section 5382 of title 5section 207(c)(2)(A) of title 18

children.heading: Bonus
children.label: B Bonus
children.text: In addition, the Chief Operating Officer may receive a bonus in an amount that does not exceed 50 percent of such annual rate of basic pay, based upon the Secretary’s evaluation of the Chief Operating Officer’s performance in relation to the goals set forth in the performance agreement described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Payment
children.label: C Payment
children.text: Payment of a bonus under subparagraph (B) may be made to the Chief Operating Officer only to the extent that such payment does not cause the Chief Operating Officer’s total aggregate compensation in a calendar year to equal or exceed the amount of the President’s salary under .section 102 of title 3

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer may appoint such senior managers as that officer determines necessary without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: B Compensation
children.text: The senior managers described in subparagraph (A) may be paid without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of such title relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates.

children.heading: Performance agreement
children.label: 2 Performance agreement
children.text: Each year, the Chief Operating Officer and each senior manager appointed under this subsection shall enter into an annual performance agreement that sets forth measurable organization and individual goals. The agreement shall be subject to review and renegotiation at the end of each term.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A senior manager appointed under this subsection may be paid at an annual rate of basic pay of not more than the maximum rate of basic pay for the Senior Executive Service under , including any applicable locality-based comparability payment that may be authorized under section 5304(h)(2)(C) of such title. The compensation of a senior manager shall be considered for purposes of to be the equivalent of that described under clause (ii) of section 207(c)(2)(A) of such title.section 5382 of title 5section 207(c)(2)(A) of title 18

children.heading: Bonus
children.label: B Bonus
children.text: In addition, a senior manager may receive a bonus in an amount such that the manager’s total annual compensation does not exceed 125 percent of the maximum rate of basic pay for the Senior Executive Service, including any applicable locality-based comparability payment, based upon the Chief Operating Officer’s evaluation of the manager’s performance in relation to the goals set forth in the performance agreement described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Removal
children.label: 4 Removal
children.text: A senior manager shall be removable by the Chief Operating Officer, or by the Secretary if the position of Chief Operating Officer is vacant.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer, in consultation with the Secretary, shall appoint a Student Loan Ombudsman to provide timely assistance to borrowers of loans made, insured, or guaranteed under subchapter IV by performing the functions described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: Public information
children.label: 2 Public information
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer shall disseminate information about the availability and functions of the Ombudsman to students, borrowers, and potential borrowers, as well as institutions of higher education, lenders, guaranty agencies, loan servicers, and other participants in those student loan programs.

children.label: A
children.text: in accordance with regulations of the Secretary, receive, review, and attempt to resolve informally complaints from borrowers of loans described in paragraph (1), including, as appropriate, attempts to resolve such complaints within the Department of Education and with institutions of higher education, lenders, guaranty agencies, loan servicers, and other participants in the loan programs described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: compile and analyze data on borrower complaints and make appropriate recommendations.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 4 Report
children.text: Each year, the Ombudsman shall submit a report to the Chief Operating Officer, for inclusion in the annual report under subsection (c)(2), that describes the activities, and evaluates the effectiveness of the Ombudsman during the preceding year.

children.heading: Personnel ceilings
children.label: 1 Personnel ceilings
children.text: The PBO shall not be subject to any ceiling relating to the number or grade of employees.

children.heading: Administrative flexibility
children.label: 2 Administrative flexibility
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer shall work with the Office of Personnel Management to develop and implement personnel flexibilities in staffing, classification, and pay that meet the needs of the PBO, subject to compliance with title 5.

children.heading: Excepted service
children.label: 3 Excepted service
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer may appoint, without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service, technical and professional employees to administer the functions of the PBO. These employees may be paid without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of such title relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates.

children.heading: Establishment of fair and equitable system for measuring staff performance
children.label: h Establishment of fair and equitable system for measuring staff performance
children.text: The PBO shall establish an annual performance management system, subject to compliance with title 5, and consistent with applicable provisions of law and regulations, which strengthens the effectiveness of the PBO by providing for establishing goals or objectives for individual, group, or organizational performance (or any combination thereof), consistent with the performance plan of the PBO and its performance planning procedures, including those established under the Government Performance and Results Act of 1993, and communicating such goals or objectives to employees.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: i Authorization of appropriations
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate from funds made available under such funds as are appropriate to the functions assumed by the PBO. In addition, there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this part.section 1087h of this title
